name: Mailbox
description:
Library component. Models the bead store as the persistent handoff mailbox. The mailbox holds the agent's saved state across a respawn -- the single source of truth bridging the predecessor agent's death and the successor's boot. Real implementation: sendHandoffMail (internal/cmd/handoff.go:515) writes a self-addressed bead with the auto-collected state; the successor's gt prime --hook reads it via runPrimeCompactResume (prime.go:167-170).

The mailbox sequences delivery: it persists mail when the cycle saves, but only delivers AFTER the pane has respawned and signaled that the successor process exists. This captures the substrate ordering -- the mail can't be read by a process that hasn't booted yet.

v0.2 (defense-doc P2.4): mailbox now loops Delivered -> NoMail so subsequent substrate cycles can persist fresh mail.

SAVE.description: Handoff cycle persists the agent's state. Real: sendHandoffMail writes to the beads database; auto-hook ensures the successor sees it as their hooked work.

BOOT_TRIGGER.description: Pane signals that the successor process has started. Mailbox now has someone to deliver to. Real: when the new pane runs SessionStart hook, gt prime --hook initiates the resume.

DELIVER.description: Successor agent's SessionStart hook reads the persisted mail and resumes. Real: gt prime --hook -> runPrimeCompactResume injects the mail context into the fresh Claude process.

actors.name: Mailbox
actors.owner: Persistent state-handoff store. Spec-side abstraction of the beads database row whose life spans the pane respawn (the only state that survives the kill). Loops Delivered -> NoMail so multi-cycle traces can run.

triples.event: pane respawn complete -- successor process has started
successor agent's SessionStart hook reads the mail
mail consumed; reset for next cycle
